free_text: Dipole response of 88Sr up to the neutron-separation
energy
Universite des Sciences et de la
Technologie d'Alger,Bab-Ezzouar-Alger,Algeria
Science and Technology University of Algeria,
Bab-Ezzouar-Alger, Algeria
Experiment was performed at the
superconducting electron accelerator ELBE of the
Research Center Dresden-Rossendorf
The target consisted of 2731.8 mg of 88SrCO3
enriched to 99.9%, combined with 455.5 mg of 11B used
for the determination of the photon flux.
Scattered photons were measured with four high-
purity germanium (HPGe) detectors of about 100%
efficiency relative to a 3 x 3 in. NaI detector.
Two HPGe detectors were placed at theta = 90 deg
with respect to the incident photon beam at a
distance of 28 cm to the target while the other two
were positioned at theta= 127 deg at a distance of
32 cm to the target, corresponding to opening angles
of 16 and 14 deg, respectively.
The target was combined
with 445.5 mg of 11B.
Bremsstrahlung was produced by irradiating
niobium foils of 4 mum or 7 mum thickness with
electron beams with energies of 9.0, 13.2 and 16.0 MeV
and average currents of 520, 400, and 420 micro A
hitting radiators consisting of niobium foils of
4, 7, and 2 micro m thickness, respectively.
no information given
